summary: Gruber highlights Meta's reported plans to cut 10-30% of its Metaverse division, shifting investment toward augmented reality glasses instead. He frames this as vindication for skeptics who never bought into Zuckerberg's 2021 'metaverse-first' rebrand, when Facebook became Meta with grand promises about bringing the metaverse to life. Gruber contrasts Zuckerberg's original vision statement with the reality of the retreat, and recalls Greg Joswiak's memorable dismissal of the metaverse as 'a word I'll never use.' He also cites Om Malik's observation that the rename was really about escaping Facebook's toxic brand, not genuine metaverse conviction, and closes with his own earlier assessment that Meta is 'the perfect name for a company that believes in nothing other than its own success.'
key_points: Meta is cutting 10-30% of its Metaverse unit and redirecting spending toward AR glasses, effectively abandoning the vision that justified its 2021 rebrand
Zuckerberg's 2021 declaration that Meta would be 'metaverse-first, not Facebook-first' looks absurd just four years later
Apple executives publicly dismissed the metaverse concept well before Vision Pro launched, and that skepticism proved correct
The Facebook-to-Meta rebrand was primarily about escaping a toxic brand association with misinformation, not genuine belief in the metaverse
Meta's willingness to abandon its defining vision confirms it as a company driven by opportunism rather than conviction
money_quotes: I'm so old that I remember ... checks notes ... four years ago, when Facebook renamed itself Meta.
From now on, we will be metaverse-first, not Facebook-first.
The metaverse is... A word I'll never use.
The reason Mark renamed the company is because the Facebook brand was becoming toxic, and associated with misinformation and global-scale crap.

And so while 'Meta' will never be remembered as the company that spearheaded the metaverse — because the metaverse never was or will be an actual thing — it's in truth the perfect name for a company that believes in nothing other than its own success.
key_insight: Meta's retreat from the metaverse confirms what skeptics always knew: the rebrand was never about a genuine technological vision, but about a company that believes in nothing other than its own success.
